---
title: "Publish Your Data"
topics:
  - Workflows
  - Publishing & Open Science
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

```{=html}
<div class="status-badge-row">
  <span class="status-badge status-active">Active Workflow</span>
  <span class="status-badge status-canonical">Validation Required</span>
  <span class="status-badge status-guidance">Internal + External Paths</span>
</div>
```

Use this page when your dataset is nearly ready and you need to choose the correct **release path**.

## Before publishing anything

Confirm this chain is complete:

1. ontology mappings are finalized
2. the salmon data package is complete in `metadata/` + `data/`
3. validation is clean
4. provenance, contacts, and version notes are present
5. required approvals are in place

## Choose the destination path

### Internal / SPSR path

Use this when the dataset is moving into FSAR-oriented operational workflows.

- keep the **salmon data package** as the source of truth
- choose the SPSR route scope that matches the data grain:
  - **CU/composite**
  - **SMU**
  - **Population**
- generate wizard or bulk upload files from the same package
- do not maintain a separate spreadsheet-only bulk path

Canonical guidance:

- [Salmon data package + guided review + SPSR intake path](ontology-data-package-gpt.qmd)
- [FSAR Data Standardization Workflow](../../how_to_guides/fsar-data-standardization-workflow.qmd)

### External / public release path

Use this when the dataset is approved for external publication.

- follow classification and approval requirements
- include citation, version, provenance, and licence information
- document how the public release relates to any internal operational package

Canonical guidance:

- [Publishing Data Externally](../publishing_data_externally.qmd)

## Release checklist

- [ ] package metadata is complete and validated
- [ ] release scope is clear (internal SPSR, external publication, or both)
- [ ] route scope is documented for SPSR submissions
- [ ] upload or release artifacts trace back to the same package version
- [ ] approvals and contact information are current
- [ ] release notes are archived

## Cookbook spotlight

If your near-term publishing task is a CU/composite escapement submission, use the focused recipe:

- [CU/composite escapement estimates for package-first SPSR intake](../../cookbook/cu-composite-escapement-estimates.qmd)

## Next Steps

- [FSAR Data Standardization Workflow](../../how_to_guides/fsar-data-standardization-workflow.qmd)
- [Salmon data package + guided review + SPSR intake path](ontology-data-package-gpt.qmd)
- [Publishing Data Externally](../publishing_data_externally.qmd)
